title: A still pond: you allowed to be the surface
subtitle: A ten-minute bedtime visualisation for practising stillness without needing to do anything with it.
excerpt: A ten-minute visualisation for bedtime. The image is a still pond. You are the surface: not deep, not active, just still. For when stillness feels like an impossible state rather than a natural one.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education, developed for the safety-in-stillness theme of Day 5 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025). Water imagery for rest and stillness is a public-domain contemplative technique.

practice.whenToUse: Use in bed when stillness feels unnatural and you want an image to hold instead of a blank mind.

content.text: A ten-minute visualisation for bedtime. The image is not an instruction to relax: it is a picture of what stillness already looks like. You hold the image and let the body follow.
Lie down. Eyes closed. Three slow breaths.
Picture a pond. Not a dramatic view of a pond: just the surface of it, close. Early morning or late evening. No wind. The surface is completely flat.
Now notice that you are the surface of the pond. Not the water underneath, not the depths. Just the surface: flat, still, receiving the light.

When a thought arrives it is like a leaf touching the surface. It makes a small ripple. Notice the ripple without following it. Watch it move to the edges of the image and settle. The surface returns to flat.
Hold the image of the still surface. Flat. Receiving light. Nothing to do except be the surface.
Stay with the image for ten minutes, or let sleep arrive from it.

Original to homemade.education, developed for the safety-in-stillness theme of Day 5 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025). Water imagery for rest and present-moment awareness draws on a long public-domain contemplative tradition.
